DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-10-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT PURSUANT TO CCP 430.10.

Appearances by Zoom: Jaynry Mak (415.307.9783) for defendants and moving parties; Abimbola S. Moses of the Estavillo Law Group (510.982.3001) for plaintiffs and opposing parties.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. The complaint as alleged is uncertain. Multiple allegations are made regarding property transfers obtained by fraud and manipulation from Mui-Tai Vong, as trustee and as an individual starting with Grant Deed attached as Exhibit C to the complaint (Complaint, paragraph 16-18). However, in opposition Plaintiffs assert that they desire to quiet title based on the same Grant Deed attached as Exhibit C (which was purportedly obtained by fraud and manipulation) (See Opposition 5:24-26). Plaintiffs also assert that the estate of Mui-Tai Vong is not a necessary party to this action even though the operative Deed according to the Plaintiffs is the one vesting title in Mui-Tai Vong, an individual.

Plaintiff further asserts that the statute of limitations does not bar this action because one of the Plaintiffs, signatory to the 2010 Agreement, doesn't read or write English. Plaintiffs fail to explain the relevancy of this assertion as it pertains to the statute of limitations based on the governing authorities re: contracts and torts.

Plaintiff further claims that the probate proceeding has been resolved, however the Probate matter is still pending.

Therefore, the demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to clearly allege the basis for quiet title cause of action, the date as of which title is being quieted, what interest and in whom is being quieted. Furthermore, Plaintiff to allege facts addressing the statute of limitations.

Additionally, this matter shall be stayed until the final resolution of the pending probate case and the First Amended Complaint shall be filed and served within 30 days of the final resolution of the Probate matter.

The Court does not address any other arguments raised in the demurrer at this time pending the clarifications in the amended complaint.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)
